utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all details (including costs). 1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan includes both 'physical' and 'mental' entries with named plans and monthly costs, so fully specified. 2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides 'EliteHome Premium Coverage (€95/month)', includes cost, but does not explicitly state that it covers home/contents; however the label implies property coverage, and the requirement is 'home/contents insurance with costs'—the item name matches property_coverage and includes cost, but not an explicit breakdown into home vs contents; still likely acceptable as a property policy with cost. 3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs: plan includes 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan (€115/month)', so specified. 4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs: two services listed with monthly costs, so specified. 5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown: plan provides '€495/month' only; no annual cost provided, so not a complete monthly/annual breakdown. Therefore, 4 items are fully specified; total_budget is incomplete.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health, LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health, EliteHome Premium Coverage for property, PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel, Adventure Travel Coverage add-on, and Technology Protection add-on. Using the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health) rating 9; LifeFirst Standard Plan (mental health) rating 4; EliteHome Premium Coverage rating 2; PrimeTravel Premium Plan rating 3; Adventure Travel Coverage rating 10; Technology Protection rating 8.
